title: Create TierClassifier service (app/services/tier_classifier.py)
context: Part of task-tier-at-creation initiative. Tier classification currently happens mid-workflow in a `classify_model_tier` node. We need it at task creation time instead. See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md

spec.function: async def classify_task_tier(payload: dict, db: AsyncSession) -> str
logic: 1. If payload['model_tier'] is non-empty -> return it unchanged

2. Project hard minimums: if project_id contains 'lobs-server', 'lobs-mission-control', or 'lobs-mobile' -> minimum 'standard'. Read overrides from DB setting 'model_router.project_minimums' (dict of project_id -> min_tier). Fallback to hardcoded lobs-* defaults.
spec.imports: Use aiohttp for LM Studio call. Import MODEL_ROUTER_TIER_MICRO_KEY from app.orchestrator.model_router. Import OrchestratorSetting from app.models.

acceptance: Unit tests: (a) caller-set tier passes through unchanged, (b) lobs-server project gets minimum standard even if LLM returns small, (c) LM Studio timeout/unavailable falls back to standard, (d) LLM returns 'small' for a script task. All tests pass.

title: Wire tier classification into POST /api/tasks
context: Part of task-tier-at-creation initiative. After TierClassifier service exists, integrate it into task creation. See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md

spec.location: In create_task(), after 'await db.flush()' and 'await db.refresh(db_task)', before the final return
spec.change: If db_task.model_tier is None or empty, call `tier = await classify_task_tier(payload, db)` and set `db_task.model_tier = tier`. Then call `await db.flush()` again so the tier is persisted. Import classify_task_tier from app.services.tier_classifier.
spec.note: The classification must happen before the return — the TaskCreated ControlLoopEvent is already emitted at flush time, but that's OK; the orchestrator reads task.model_tier fresh from DB when it processes the event.

acceptance: POST /api/tasks with no model_tier returns task with model_tier set (not null). POST with model_tier='strong' returns model_tier='strong' unchanged. Tests pass.

title: ModelChooser: short-circuit to tier list when task.model_tier is set
context: Part of task-tier-at-creation initiative. Currently ModelChooser.choose() always calls decide_models() which does fuzzy task analysis, ignoring task.model_tier. We need a direct path: model_tier -> DB tier list -> candidates. See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md

changes: Add helper at module level: def _tier_to_lane(tier: str) -> str — maps micro/small -> 'background', medium/standard -> 'standard', strong -> 'critical', default -> 'standard'

In ModelChooser.choose(), after `tiers = dict(cfg.get('tiers') or {})` and after local model discovery injection, add: task_tier = (task.get('model_tier') or '').strip().lower(). If task_tier is non-empty AND task_tier in tiers AND tiers[task_tier] is non-empty list: set candidates = list(tiers[task_tier]) and budget_lane = _tier_to_lane(task_tier). Skip the decide_models() call entirely.
If task_tier is not set or tier list is empty, fall through to existing decide_models() path as-is.

For the short-circuit path, the audit dict should include: {'tier_source': 'task.model_tier', 'tier': task_tier, 'tier_models': tiers}. Pass candidates into the existing routing policy, budget guards, and health ranking steps unchanged.
For ModelChoice construction in the short-circuit path, use detected_lane from lane_guard_decision.get('lane') as usual (budget guard may override), and set audit tier_models to the full tiers dict.

acceptance: Unit test: mock DB returning tier lists, call choose() with task={'model_tier': 'small'} -> first candidate comes from small tier list. Call with task={} (no tier) -> existing decide_models path runs. All existing model chooser tests still pass.

title: Fix worker.py _terminate_session: remove broken sessions_kill call
context: worker.py._terminate_session() calls sessions_kill via Gateway, which doesn't exist (verified: returns 'Tool not available'). Sessions auto-archive after archiveAfterMinutes=5. The fix: make _terminate_session a no-op like workflow_nodes.py.delete_session(). See design: docs/task-tier-and-session-cleanup-design.md

spec.change: Replace the entire body of _terminate_session() with: logger.debug('[WORKER] Session %s will be auto-archived by OpenClaw. reason=%s', session_key, reason); return True. Remove the aiohttp import from this method if it's only used here (check other usages first).
spec.note: The method signature stays the same so callers don't need to change.

acceptance: No sessions_kill in codebase after change. _terminate_session returns True. No HTTP calls made. Run grep -rn sessions_kill app/ to verify. Tests pass.
